title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind
author: William Kamkwamba & Bryan Mealer

setting.note: The maize country of central Malawi, where the Kasungu plateau rolls east from the district town toward the trading centre of Wimbe. Through the 2001-02 famine a teenager on his family's farm teaches himself from library books and builds a windmill out of scrap, turning wind into light and water. The map runs from the farm and the little school at Wimbe out to the district town, and finally south to the capital.

palette.note: The green of a maize field standing after the rains come back, against the drought that opens the story. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
